questions.question: Which of the following scenarios best represents the advantage of Divide and Conquer algorithms like Merge Sort or Quick Sort?

answers.a: When the dataset is very small
answers.b: When only one comparison is needed
answers.d: When problems can be broken into independent smaller parts

explanations.a: Divide and Conquer is not specifically for small datasets.
explanations.c: Recursion is essential for Divide and Conquer.
explanations.d: Correct — it works best when problems can be divided into smaller independent sub-problems.

questions.question: What is the primary reason for using sorting algorithms in computer science?

answers.b: To organize data for efficient searching and processing

explanations.a: Sorting does not primarily reduce memory usage.
explanations.b: Correct — sorted data improves searching and processing efficiency.
explanations.c: Sorting does not directly improve security.

questions.question: What is the time complexity of Merge Sort in the worst case?

explanations.b: Quadratic time applies to simpler sorts.
explanations.c: Correct — Merge Sort runs in O(n log n).

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the divide and conquer technique?

answers.a: Dividing a problem into smaller sub-problems, solving them, and combining the results
answers.b: Solving a problem iteratively using loops
answers.c: Sorting elements by comparing adjacent values
answers.d: Solving a problem by trying all possible solutions

explanations.a: Correct — divide and conquer splits, solves, and combines results.

questions.question: In Quick Sort, what is the role of the pivot element?

answers.a: It stores the final sorted array
answers.b: It determines the size of the input array
answers.c: It is always the smallest element
answers.d: It divides the array into two subarrays based on comparison

explanations.a: Pivot does not store the sorted array.
explanations.c: Pivot is not always the smallest.
